About Me

From Physiology to Healthcare Intelligence

3+
Years in Healthcare Analytics
1M+
Records Analyzed at LASHMA
40%
Reporting Cycle Reduction
MPH
Epidemiology — Uni of Lagos

My path into healthcare intelligence began with a B.Sc. in Physiology — a discipline that trains you to think in systems, understand interdependencies, and ask why things behave the way they do. That same mindset transfers directly to healthcare data: complex, interconnected, and full of signals that most organizations never surface.

I built my analytical foundation at St. Mary's Catholic General Hospital, administering electronic health records for 500,000+ patient profiles and developing the operational dashboards that cut reporting time from days to hours. That's where I learned that data quality isn't a technical detail — it's what determines whether a clinical decision gets made with confidence or guesswork.

"At LASHMA, I managed and analyzed over 1 million enrollee and claims records — delivering the utilization intelligence that shaped executive strategy and health policy for Lagos State's health insurance scheme."

At Lagos State Health Management Agency (LASHMA), I built and deployed enterprise Power BI dashboards that reduced manual reporting cycles by 40%, designed SQL-based ETL pipelines for automated data transformation, and produced analytical briefs for senior leadership that translated raw claims data into strategic decisions.

Open to Healthcare Data Analyst roles in the UK, EU, US, Canada, and remote-first global organizations.

Data Storytelling

Turning Numbers Into Narratives

Healthcare data only creates value when it's interpreted and communicated clearly. Here are key insights from my analytical work.

01

Why Hospital Wait Times Spike During Peak Periods

Analysis of patient flow data across 18 months revealed that wait-time spikes aren't random — they follow predictable patterns tied to staff shift changes, seasonal admission surges, and bed-turnover delays in specific wards.

→ Predictable bottlenecks can be pre-empted with scheduling intelligence.
02

What Drives Claims Delays in Healthcare Systems

Claims delay analysis across provider networks identified that 68% of delays originate from three root causes: incomplete documentation at submission, mismatched diagnostic codes, and manual review queues with no triage priority system.

→ Process redesign at submission stage reduces average cycle time by 30%.
03

Key Predictors of Patient No-Shows

Predictive modeling across appointment data revealed that lead time beyond 14 days, first-visit patients, and afternoon scheduling are the strongest independent predictors of non-attendance.

→ Targeted reminder interventions on high-risk slots reduce no-shows by 22%.
04

Operational Inefficiencies Hidden in Healthcare Workflows

Mapping operational data against process timelines in a multi-specialty facility revealed that discharge delays — not admission volume — were the primary driver of bed unavailability.

→ Discharge planning KPIs are the most undertracked metric in hospital ops.

Public Health Expertise

Analytics Grounded in Public Health Systems

My MPH background isn't an academic footnote — it's the lens through which I interpret healthcare data. Population-level thinking changes how you design a dashboard, what KPIs you choose, and what story you tell with the numbers.

📊

Population Health Analytics

Designing insights that account for demographic, geographic, and social determinants — not just clinical variables.

📋

M&E Frameworks

Monitoring and evaluation systems aligned to healthcare program logic models — measuring what actually matters.

🏥

Evidence-Based Reporting

Translating public health evidence into actionable intelligence for healthcare administrators and policymakers.

"Healthcare analytics without public health context produces answers to the wrong questions. Data gains meaning when it's anchored to populations, systems, and outcomes — not just transactions."

— Core philosophy driving every project
